knot_assessments.notes: Proper surgeon's knot with double throw executed effectively
Single throw with proper hand switch and adequate tension
Final single throw with hand switch, securing knot well

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain slit appears well closed with no visible gaps

technique_summary: Excellent demonstration of proper FLS Task 5 technique with correct surgeon's knot formation, appropriate hand switching between throws, and efficient completion time. Both teachers agreed on technical execution quality and penalty assessments. Major disagreement was on completion time (45s vs 120s) - consensus favors the more realistic 45-second assessment given the efficient technique observed. The trainee showed good control and efficiency in needle handling and knot tying.
improvement_suggestions: Continue practicing to maintain consistency in suture placement accuracy
Practice precise suture placement to reduce deviation from target marks
Focus on minimizing any small deviations from target marks
